report_name: Nightspire Ransomware Group Claims Attack on Italian Firm Pat S.r.l

threat_actors.name: Nightspire
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group operating a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) model that uses double extortion tactics. Claimed responsibility for the attack on Pat S.r.l.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of victim organizations through data encryption and the threat of leaking exfiltrated data.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Used by the Nightspire group to steal sensitive data from the victim's network before encryption, as part of their double extortion strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Used by the Nightspire group to encrypt files on the victim's network to cause operational disruption and extort a ransom.

malware.name: Nightspire
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Nightspire group to encrypt victim files as part of their double extortion attacks.

identities.name: Pat S.r.l
identities.description: An Italian company targeted by the Nightspire ransomware group and added to their data leak site.

infrastructure.name: Nightspire Leak Site
infrastructure.description: The dark web data leak site operated by the Nightspire group to publish names of victims and threaten the release of exfiltrated data.

indicators.name: AdFind Execution for Reconnaissance
indicators.pattern:
title: AdFind Execution for Reconnaissance
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e4f-4b1a-9c8d-6f5e4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of AdFind, a tool often used by adversaries for Active Directory reconnaissance.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/blog/nightspire-targets-pat-s-r-l-in-confirmed-ransomware-incident
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1018
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\adfind.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of AdFind, a legitimate command-line tool for querying Active Directory, often used by attackers for network reconnaissance as suggested in the hunting hints.

indicators.name: Mimikatz Execution for Credential Dumping
indicators.pattern:
title: Mimikatz Execution for Credential Dumping
id: 1e8d7c6b-5a4f-4e3d-8c2b-1a9f8e7d6c5b
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz, a tool widely used for credential dumping.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/blog/nightspire-targets-pat-s-r-l-in-confirmed-ransomware-incident
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.credential_access
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mimikatz.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz, a well-known credential dumping tool, as suggested in the hunting hints for pre-ransomware activity.

indicators.name: Domain Admin Group Discovery via Net Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Domain Admin Group Discovery via Net Command
id: 7d6c5b4a-3e2d-4c1b-9a8f-5e4d3c2b1a9f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the 'net' command to enumerate members of the 'Domain Admins' group, a common reconnaissance technique.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/blog/nightspire-targets-pat-s-r-l-in-confirmed-ransomware-incident
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1069.002
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'group'
      - 'Domain Admins'
      - '/domain'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the 'net' command to enumerate privileged accounts in a domain, a key step in privilege escalation, as suggested in the hunting hints.

indicators.name: PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass
id: 3c2b1a9f-8e7d-4c6b-9a5f-1e8d7c6b5a4f
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell being launched with the execution policy bypass flag, which can be used to run malicious scripts.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/blog/nightspire-targets-pat-s-r-l-in-confirmed-ransomware-incident
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-25
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -exec bypass'
      - ' -ExecutionPolicy Bypass'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of PowerShell with an execution policy bypass, a common technique for running unsigned or malicious scripts, as suggested in the hunting hints.

relationships.source_name: Domain Admin Group Discovery via Net Command
